/* Abstract algebra */ Fixing wording on the part of the paragraph talking about inverse elements. While I am no professional mathematician, I am familiar with professional mathematics' idiomatic vocab. For example, it would make more sense for -3 to 'undo' 3 versus -3 to 'reverse the effects' of 3 and the vocab being the same with {-3, 3} being replaced with {a, a^-1}. Same with a ⚬ a^-1 being such that a 'operates' on a^-1, not a being 'inked' with a^-1.

/* Definition and etymology */ try rephrasing this bit again. saying that this book title is translated as «Modern English translation» is confusing in this context, because we are trying explain how the word "algebra" came to be common, which happened many centuries prior to such a translation
